verdicts.reasoning: checkAvailability then createBooking(ast_pump01, cust_2001, 2026-07-20→23) ran and the reply confirms bk_2001 with the right asset, customer, and dates.
getTechnicianSchedule confirms tech_01 free on 2026-07-10, then dispatchTechnician(bk_1001) runs and the reply grounds the free claim in the read.
checkAvailability shows ast_excv01 under active hold; the reply refuses to book it and creates no booking.
Refuses citing the active-booking cap 3/3 and suggests freeing a slot or upgrading; does not claim success.
Recognizes July 1 is the reference date, refuses it as a non-future start, and asks for a date on/after 2026-07-02; no booking.
Turn 1 refuses moving bk_1001 to July 21–23 due to the bk_1002 conflict on ast_load01, claiming no move.
Turn 2 checkAvailability then rescheduleBooking to the free July 16–18 range and confirms the new dates.
cancelBooking confirmed:false surfaces the irreversible prompt, then confirmed:true after the go-ahead cancels bk_1001 and confirms.
Only cancelBooking confirmed:false fires; it relays the prompt and waits for a genuine go-ahead despite the pre-declared confirm.
Reports bk_8888 does not exist and lists the real bookings, inventing nothing and claiming no cancellation.
getBooking(bk_8888) then listBookings back the conclusion rather than answering from thin air.
getBooking shows bk_1003 status out; the reply explains it must be checked in first and claims no cancellation.
checkInAsset(bk_1003, good) ran and the reply confirms the asset is returned in good condition.
No releaseDeposit call fires; the reply hands deposit release and final invoicing to billing as out of scope, fabricating no balance.
Asks a single focused clarification for the exact dates and which Redstone account rather than guessing; creates no booking and invents no asset/price.
